description: Canonical C4 structural model of the shopsystem framework (lead shop + BC landscape + inter-shop protocol). §3.3 artifact, owned by the Architect.

people.description: Drives the lead shop: expresses intent, dispatches work, reconciles. §3 'where stakeholders meet the work'.
people.name: Operator / Stakeholder

relationships.description: Expresses intent; dispatches work; reconciles results
Operates the lead shop (router + PO/Architect subagents)

softwareSystems.description: Source repos (dstengle/*), Actions CI, releases (version tags). Each BC = one repo (§4).
softwareSystems.name: GitHub

relationships.description: Release-tag CI publishes images

softwareSystems.description: OCI registry for published images: bc-base, templates, devcontainer (ADR-021/022; features/templates 142, devcontainer 16).
softwareSystems.name: GHCR (ghcr.io/dstengle)

components.description: Authors intent: brief, PDRs, Gherkin scenarios; answers clarify on scope/vocabulary (§3.1).
components.name: Product Owner (PO)

components.description: Owns shape, scenario assignment, reconciliation; sends assign_scenarios/request_bugfix/request_maintenance; answers clarify on architecture; maintains THIS workspace (§3.1/§3.2).
components.name: Architect

components.description: Main-agent: classifies requests, dispatches PO/Architect subagents, arms Monitor on shop-msg watch, drains pending (lead-primer).
components.name: Router

components.description: Brief, PDRs, ADRs, this structurizr workspace, Domain & Context Map, canonical Gherkin (features/), scenario-to-BC assignment, lead beads (§3.3).
components.name: Product artifacts

containers.description: Outward face of the product; owns all product-level artifacts; routes to PO/Architect subagents. Addressed as shopsystem/lead (ADR-020 D1).
containers.name: Lead shop (shopsystem-product)

relationships.description: assign_scenarios / request_bugfix / request_maintenance / request_scenario_register / request_shop_card; reads work_done/clarify
Dispatches scenario work; tightenings (ADR-019)
Dispatches role-template / bootstrap / image-publish work
Dispatches launch + bc-base rebuild work (ADR-021/022)
Dispatches adoption-doc scenarios (ADR-008)
Dispatches harness scenarios (ADR-002 dogfooding)
Will dispatch product scenarios once instantiated (PLANNED)
Reads lead inbox; deposits dispatches (via shop-msg)
Lead beads = canonical work_id; reconciliation close-out (§6)
Composes assign_scenarios / request_bugfix / request_maintenance

components.description: Outbound vehicles (assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_maintenance) and BC responses (work_done, clarify, mechanism_observation, nudge); consume releases lead-inbox slot (§5.3, ADR-009).
components.name: shop-msg send/respond/consume

relationships.description: Validates message against the catalog contract (§5.6)
Resolves --bc/--lead name -> <system>/<name> address (ADR-020)
Deposits the mailbox row (postgres-second, ADR-012)
Fires paired bd side effect (ADR-016, ADR-012 atomicity)
Delegates scenario-hash to `scenarios.hash` (in-process; ADR-019 D2)

components.description: Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY wakeup on the lead/BC inbox; upserts bc_presence on a tick (ADR-014). bc-status classifies online/stale/offline.
components.name: shop-msg watch + heartbeat

relationships.description: LISTEN/NOTIFY on inbox; upserts bc_presence on tick (ADR-014)

components.description: The 7 message types + ScenarioPayload value object; from/to required; schema is the contract (§5.2/§5.6, ADR-006 §5).
components.name: Catalog schemas (Pydantic)

relationships.description: ScenarioPayload carries a scenario whose hash rule scenarios owns (ADR-019)

components.description: Maps canonical name -> abstract <system>/<name> address; lead is authoritative writer; no filesystem path (ADR-006, ADR-020 D1/D2).
components.name: Name registry

relationships.description: Registry + messages co-located in one postgres DB (ADR-006 D1)

components.description: Fires the paired bd side effect for every shop-msg command with a bd correlate, under ADR-012 atomicity (ADR-016).
components.name: bd integration facade

relationships.description: bd create/update/close via CLI (ADR-016 D4)

components.description: messages table keyed by recipient address; inbox/outbox rows; (work_id,direction,shop) uniqueness; bc_presence table (ADR-006, ADR-012, ADR-014).
components.name: Mailbox storage

containers.description: The protocol hub. Owns shop-msg, the postgres mailbox, the name registry, bd integration, presence heartbeat. Subdomain: Inter-shop coordination (ADR-002).
containers.name: Messaging BC (shopsystem-messaging)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify / mechanism_observation / nudge -> lead inbox
Owns + reads/writes messages, shop_registry, bc_presence
shop-msg owns bd integration: paired side effect per command (ADR-016)
Cuts version-tag releases; CI builds/tests
Delegates scenario-hash to `scenarios.hash` (in-process; ADR-019 D2)

containers.description: Owns canonicalization + the `scenarios hash`/`verify` contract tool — the single source of truth for what a scenario hash IS (scenario-block-only). Subdomain: Specification (ADR-002/019).
containers.name: Scenarios BC (shopsystem-scenarios)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify -> lead inbox
Reads inbox / writes responses (via shop-msg)
Cuts version-tag releases; CI builds/tests (scenario 129 convention)

containers.description: Owns role templates (shop-templates show/list), shop bootstrap + update, and image publish-on-tag. Subdomain: Role discipline (ADR-002; features/templates).
containers.name: Templates BC (shopsystem-templates)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify -> lead inbox
Reads inbox / writes responses (via shop-msg)
Publishes templates + devcontainer images on version tag (features/templates 142, devcontainer 16)
Cuts version-tag releases; CI builds/tests

containers.description: Owns `bc-container` (launch/attach/inject/monitor/stop/status/list) and the bc-base image (Dockerfile + publish + centralized rebuild). Sets SHOPMSG_DSN in containers. Subdomain: Platform Operations (ADR-004/021/022).
containers.name: BC Launcher (shopsystem-bc-launcher)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify -> lead inbox
Reads inbox / writes responses (via shop-msg)
Gates launch on messaging DB reachability (features/bc-launcher/33)
Pulls :latest bc-base at launch; publishes rebuilt bc-base (ADR-021 D3 / ADR-022)
Watches releases; bakes `scenarios` CLI into bc-base (ADR-022 D2)
Watches releases; bakes `shop-templates` into bc-base (ADR-022 D2)
Polls dep release tags with own GITHUB_TOKEN; hosts bc-base build (ADR-022 D2/D3)
Scheduled/workflow_dispatch: bump Dockerfile pins, rebuild bc-base if changed (ADR-022 D2/D4)

containers.description: End-user adoption documentation (v1 plain markdown). Disjoint adopter audience. Subdomain: Platform Operations / adoption (ADR-008; features/docs).
containers.name: Docs BC (shopsystem-docs)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify -> lead inbox
Reads inbox / writes responses (via shop-msg)

containers.description: Validates the framework against itself: experiment/slice/run/evidence/finding/baseline. Composes shop-msg/scenarios/shop-templates at the CLI boundary. Subdomain: Platform Operations (ADR-002; features/test-harness).
containers.name: Test Harness BC (shopsystem-test-harness)

relationships.description: work_done / clarify -> lead inbox
Reads inbox / writes responses (via shop-msg)

containers.description: First real consumer product on the framework; tracked for a future ADR-003. Not yet instantiated (ADR-002 §sequencing).
containers.name: Ecommerce product BC (PLANNED)

containers.description: The single postgres instance all shops share: messages, shop_registry, bc_presence tables. Reached via SHOPMSG_DSN. The substrate of the inter-shop channel (ADR-006/012/014/020; compose.yaml).
containers.name: Shared Postgres mailbox

relationships.description: Delivers inbox notifications to the router's Monitor (LISTEN/NOTIFY; ADR-014)

containers.description: beads-native work tracking; lead bead IDs are the canonical work_id flowing outward (§6, ADR-011/016). bd is authoritative for state, shop-msg for transport (PDR-010).
containers.name: bd work registry (per shop)

softwareSystems.description: One lead shop + N BC-shops coordinating via the typed inter-shop protocol over a shared postgres mailbox (§3, §5).
softwareSystems.name: shopsystem framework

relationships.description: Hosts repos; CI builds/tests; cuts version-tag releases
Publishes + pulls images (bc-base, templates, devcontainer)

name: shopsystem

componentViews.description: The messaging hub internals: send/respond/consume, watch+heartbeat, catalog schemas, name registry, bd facade, postgres storage. Touched by every inter-shop interaction.
componentViews.name: Component View: shopsystem framework - Messaging BC (shopsystem-messaging)

containerViews.description: Lead shop + each BC as a container, the shared postgres mailbox, and the bd registry. Edges labelled with the protocol (shop-msg over postgres).
containerViews.name: Container View: shopsystem framework

dynamicViews.description: assign_scenarios dispatch -> BC internal Implementer->Reviewer gated loop -> work_done with scenario_hashes -> lead reconciliation (§4.2/§4.4, ADR-012/016).
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: shopsystem framework

relationships.description: 1. Architect: shop-msg send assign_scenarios --bc <name> --work-id <lead-bead> (hash via scenarios)
2. bd-first then deposit inbox row (ADR-012 atomicity)
3. Implementer: run BDD; recompute scenario hashes (ADR-019)
4. Reviewer: adversarial gate — block work_done until hashes/clean-tree satisfied (§4.4)
5. shop-msg respond work_done(complete) with passing scenario_hashes -> lead inbox
6. Monitor fires <work_id> work_done; Architect reconciles register + hashes, bd close (ADR-016 D3)

dynamicViews.description: BC clarify -> lead resolves by additive re-dispatch on a NEW lead bead (ADR-009 layer b); original dispatch stays in flight.
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: shopsystem framework

relationships.description: 1. BC: shop-msg respond clarify --work-id <orig> -> lead inbox (asks scope/architecture)
2. Monitor fires <work_id> clarify; router routes scope/vocab to PO, architecture to Architect
3. PO/Architect decide; Architect picks vehicle on a NEW bead, send request_bugfix/assign_scenarios --work-id <new-bead>, citing <orig> (additive, ADR-009)
4. New dispatch deposited; original row consumed once resolving dispatch is on the wire

dynamicViews.description: Framework-utility release -> centralized bc-base rebuild in bc-launcher -> republish :latest -> launch pulls fresh digest (ADR-022 D2/D5; ADR-021 D3).
dynamicViews.name: Dynamic View: shopsystem framework

relationships.description: 1. Utility cuts a version-tag release (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) (scenario 129 convention)
2. bc-launcher scheduled/workflow_dispatch poll reads latest dep tags with own GITHUB_TOKEN (ADR-022 D2/D3)
3. Bump @vX.Y.Z pin in bc-base Dockerfile; rebuild only if a pin changed (ADR-022 D2)
4. Republish ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base:latest at new digest (ADR-021 D1)
5. Next bc-container launch PULLS current :latest, not stale cache (ADR-021 D3 / scenario 39)

systemContextViews.description: The shopsystem framework, the operator, and the external systems it depends on (GitHub, GHCR).
systemContextViews.name: System Context View: shopsystem framework
